behaviors.description: The content passed to the summary-generation step includes data retrieved from all three sources.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is cross-module handoff (three collectors -> aggregation payload -> summary step), not a single function. Real OS/network/subprocess is not required to catch this specific failure; in-process fakes for source clients are sufficient. Higher levels rejected: System is disqualified because behavior text is about payload composition, not real infra effects; Agentic/Workflow are disqualified because correctness here is input assembly, not model judgment or multi-agent sequencing.
behaviors.test_description: Wire real collection/aggregation modules together with three stubbed source adapters returning distinct markers, run report build, and assert the exact payload sent to summary-generation contains all three source sections/values.

behaviors.description: When one data source is unavailable, the report is produced with a `data unavailable` note for that section rather than aborting.
behaviors.justification: This is resilience/state-propagation across modules (source adapter error -> section fallback note -> report still produced). The failure can be reproduced with an in-process failing adapter; real network outage mechanics are not necessary for this behavior. Higher levels rejected: System is disqualified because behavior text does not require validating real timeout/socket behavior; Agentic/Workflow are disqualified because outcome does not depend on model output quality or multi-agent chains.
behaviors.test_description: Run report generation with two source stubs returning data and one source stub raising an availability error; assert report completes and includes `data unavailable` only for the failed section.

behaviors.description: The narrative summary accurately represents the key values present in the input data.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on what the real summary model generates from input data (factual selection/representation quality). Deterministic stubs would hide the actual failure mode (misrepresentation/hallucination/omission). Higher level rejected: Workflow is disqualified because behavior text covers a single model-generation judgment, not multiple agentic decisions in sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Feed representative structured inputs with known key values to the real summary-generation model and score outputs for factual inclusion/consistency against those values across repeated runs with acceptance thresholds.

behaviors.description: A complete report — from data collection through email delivery — reaches the configured recipient's inbox correctly.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly requires end-to-end delivery to a real inbox, which crosses process/network/external email infrastructure boundaries. Mocks can pass while masking SMTP/API/auth/routing issues. Higher levels rejected: Agentic is disqualified because this behavior’s pass condition is successful end-to-end delivery, not model output quality; Workflow is disqualified because it does not require validating multiple agentic decision steps.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the script end-to-end against reachable test instances/services and a real mailbox, then assert the message arrives in the configured recipient inbox with expected report structure/content envelope.

behaviors.description: When the email delivery service is unreachable, the generated report is saved to a local file and delivery is retried on the next scheduled run.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary includes real unreachable delivery endpoint behavior plus cross-run retry on scheduled execution and persisted file state. In-process mocking can miss real process/entrypoint/path/connection failure interactions. Higher levels rejected: Agentic is disqualified because behavior is transport fallback/retry logic, not model judgment; Workflow is disqualified because it is not a multi-agentic pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Run the scheduled script once with email endpoint intentionally unreachable, verify report file is persisted locally; then run next scheduled execution with endpoint restored and verify previously saved report is retried and delivered.
